title: triticum tripsacoides
intro: Triticum tripsacoides, also known as wild einkorn or wild emmer, is an ancient species of wheat that is closely related to modern emmer and einkorn wheat. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Triticum tripsacoides include its ability to lower blood sugar levels, reduce cholesterol, and improve digestion. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or dried and used in herbal supplements, but more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
